501(c)(3) Nonprofit Organization

Privacy Is a
Fundamental
Right.

ODIPA educates consumers and organizations about digital information privacy — advocating for practices and policies that protect people in an increasingly data-driven world.

Get InvolvedOur Programs
4.5BRecords exposed in data breaches in 2023 alone
79%Of consumers seriously concerned about data privacy
$9.48MAverage cost of a financial services data breach
Consumer EducationPolicy AdvocacyOriginal ResearchCommunity BuildingCorporate CertificationAwareness CampaignsOpen-Source ToolsInternational Cooperation77% Free to the Public501(c)(3) Tax ExemptConsumer EducationPolicy AdvocacyOriginal ResearchCommunity BuildingCorporate CertificationAwareness CampaignsOpen-Source ToolsInternational Cooperation77% Free to the Public501(c)(3) Tax Exempt
Our Mission

Protecting Privacy
at Every Level

ODIPA operates at the intersection of consumer awareness, business responsibility, research, and public policy — equipping all stakeholders with the knowledge to navigate today's complex data privacy landscape.

Founded as a 501(c)(3) tax-exempt nonprofit organization, ODIPA was created to fill a critical gap: no single organization was addressing data privacy comprehensively — from the individual consumer to the enterprise to the policymaker.

Our approach is grounded in the belief that privacy is a fundamental right, and that education and awareness are the most powerful tools for protecting it. We make data privacy knowledge accessible, actionable, and relevant for all Americans.

With 77% of our services delivered completely free to the public, ODIPA's model ensures that privacy protection is not a privilege of the few — but a resource available to everyone.

Consumer Education
Free courses, webinars, and workshops for the general public
Policy Advocacy
Federal and state-level legislative engagement
Research & Publications
Annual reports and briefs, free to the public
Open-Source Tools
Free privacy technology for developers and consumers
Eight Programs

Comprehensive
Privacy Coverage

77% FREE TO THE PUBLIC

Educational Outreach

29% free·8% fee-based

Public courses, online webinars, and specialized workshops for consumers, students, seniors, and community groups.

Free to publicLearn more →

Advocacy & Policy

9% of activity

Monitoring federal and state privacy legislation, submitting position papers submitted to regulators and lawmakers to strengthen consumer protections.

100% FreeLearn more →

Research & Publications

11% free·2% fee-based

Comprehensive annual reports, research briefs on emerging threats, and special reports on significant privacy developments — freely available.

Free to publicLearn more →

Community Building

8% free·2% fee-based

Annual privacy conference, local meetups, online discussion groups, and regional networking events for advocates and citizens.

Free meetupsLearn more →

Corporate Certification

4% free·5% fee-based

A rigorous certification program verifying that businesses comply with state and federal data privacy laws — creating market transparency for consumers.

Fee-basedLearn more →

Awareness Campaigns

6% free·1% fee-based

Daily social media presence, focused awareness campaigns, and annual Privacy Awareness Week — reaching consumers through digital and traditional media channels.

Free to publicLearn more →

Open-Source Development

6% free·1% fee-based

Hosting and maintaining an open-source platform where developers share privacy-protective tools and libraries — free for individuals globally.

Free platformLearn more →

International Cooperation

4% of activity

Partnering with global privacy advocacy organizations to coordinate advocacy, share best practices, and assist emerging organizations worldwide.

100% FreeLearn more →
Why It Matters

The Privacy Crisis
Is Real

Data privacy is no longer an abstract concern. The numbers tell a stark story of why ODIPA's mission matters urgently.

0.00B
Records exposed in data breaches globally in 2023 alone
0%
Of Americans seriously concerned about how their data is collected and used
$0.00M
Average cost of a data breach in financial services — highest of any industry
0yr
Consecutive years financial services has led all industries in breach costs
0+
Different US state privacy laws now in effect or pending
0%
Of ODIPA's services delivered completely free to the public we serve
Our Service Model

Built for Public
Benefit First

77%FREE
77% Free public services
19% Fee-based business services
4% Governance & overhead

These percentages describe how ODIPA delivers services — not how we spend money. A separate financial breakdown is available upon request.

  • 01
    Free Educational CoursesFREE
    Public courses, webinars, and workshops for underserved communities
  • 02
    Corporate TrainingFEE-BASED
    Privacy training for businesses and corporations. Scoped and priced per engagement. Revenue funds free public programs.
  • 03
    Certification ProgramFEE-BASED
    Priced by organization size and number of standards in scope. Independent third-party certification against applicable privacy frameworks.
  • 04
    Research & PublicationsFREE
    All annual reports, research briefs, and special publications freely available to the public.
  • 05
    Open-Source PlatformFREE
    Privacy tools and libraries available free to all individuals, developers, and community organizations.
Corporate Sponsors

Why Sponsor
ODIPA

Data privacy is no longer a compliance checkbox — it is a strategic business imperative. Sponsoring ODIPA is a public acknowledgment relationship — your organization supports our mission and we recognize you by name and logo. If you need training, certification, or research services, see our Corporate Partnership program.

Community Sponsor
Community
$2,500/yr
  • ✓Name & logo on ODIPA website sponsor page
  • ✓Acknowledgment in annual report
  • ✓IRC §513(i) sponsorship receipt
  • —Logo in email newsletter footer
  • —Logo on event & conference materials
  • —Homepage prominent placement
Become a Sponsor
Advocate Sponsor
Advocate
$5,000/yr
  • ✓Name & logo on ODIPA website sponsor page
  • ✓Acknowledgment in annual report
  • ✓IRC §513(i) sponsorship receipt
  • ✓Logo in email newsletter footer
  • —Logo on event & conference materials
  • —Homepage prominent placement

Join ODIPA

Get Involved in
the Privacy Movement

There are many ways to support ODIPA's mission — whether you're a consumer, a privacy professional, a business, or a foundation.

Donate

Your tax-deductible donation directly funds free consumer education, policy advocacy, and open-source privacy tools for the public.

Make a donation →

Volunteer

ODIPA is powered by volunteer privacy experts, researchers, developers, advocates, and educators. Share your expertise.

Apply to volunteer →

Corporate Partner

Sponsor ODIPA's work and align your brand with digital privacy leadership. Custom partnership arrangements available.

Explore partnerships →

Attend Programs

Access our free educational courses, webinars, and community meetups. Whether you're a consumer, student, or professional.

View programs →

Contribute Code

Join our open-source developer community. Help build privacy-protective tools and libraries that people depend on.

Join the community →

Get Certified

Earn ODIPA certification and demonstrate your business's compliance with state and federal privacy laws to your customers.

Start certification →
Privacy Newsletter

Stay ahead of privacy threats.

Privacy digest: breach alerts, new privacy laws, ODIPA research releases, and practical tips to protect your data. Free, always.

No spam. Unsubscribe anytime. We never sell your data. Privacy Policy

Privacy Protection
Starts Here.

Your support funds free education, independent research, open-source tools, and policy advocacy that protects millions of people's digital privacy rights.

Donate NowContact Us
Organization for Digital Information Privacy & Awareness

Educating consumers and organizations about digital information privacy — advocating for practices and policies that protect people in an increasingly data-driven world.

ODIPA is funded through individual donations, corporate sponsorships, and foundation grants.

Privacy Newsletter

Privacy digest: breach alerts, new laws, research releases.

No spam, ever. Unsubscribe any time. We never sell your data. Privacy Policy
